name: Plains
description: Flat, open grassland with scattered oak trees and tall grass. Villages and pillager outposts spawn frequently here, making it one of the most resource-rich biomes early game. Bees can be found near oak trees with hives.

name: Sunflower Plains
description: A variant of plains covered in sunflowers, the only biome where sunflowers naturally generate. The flowers provide a renewable source of yellow dye. Otherwise identical to normal plains in terrain and mob spawns.

name: Forest
description: Dense mix of oak and birch trees providing abundant wood early game. Wolves spawn here and can be tamed. The thick canopy can make navigation tricky but provides good cover from phantoms.

name: Flower Forest
description: A forest variant with large patches of nearly every flower type in the game. The best biome for collecting dye materials and finding bees. Rabbits spawn here instead of wolves.

name: Birch Forest
description: A forest composed entirely of birch trees with a bright, uniform canopy. Provides a consistent source of birch wood. The relatively open understory makes it easier to navigate than mixed forests.

name: Old Growth Birch Forest
description: Features unusually tall birch trees that grow much higher than normal birch. Trail ruins can generate here, making it worth exploring underground. Somewhat rare compared to regular birch forests.

name: Dark Forest
description: Thick canopy of dark oak trees blocks most light, allowing hostile mobs to spawn during the day. The only biome where woodland mansions generate. Huge mushrooms grow on the forest floor alongside dark oaks.

name: Jungle
description: Dense tropical biome with towering jungle trees draped in vines and cocoa pods. Jungle temples contain trapped loot. Ocelots, parrots, and pandas are exclusive to jungle biomes. Melons generate naturally here.

name: Sparse Jungle
description: A thinned-out jungle with shorter trees and more open ground. Easier to traverse than full jungle but still spawns jungle-exclusive mobs like parrots and pandas. Trail ruins generate underground here.

name: Bamboo Jungle
description: Thick bamboo stalks dominate this jungle variant, providing massive amounts of bamboo for scaffolding and fuel. Pandas spawn most frequently here. Jungle temples can still generate among the bamboo.

name: Savanna
description: Dry grassland with scattered acacia trees and tall grass. It never rains here. Villages generate with acacia wood architecture. Horses, donkeys, and armadillos spawn naturally, making it good for early transportation.

name: Savanna Plateau
description: An elevated flat-topped savanna with sheer cliff edges. Llamas and wolves spawn here in addition to standard savanna mobs. The flat terrain on top makes it a natural defensive building location. No rain falls here.

name: Windswept Hills
description: Steep, rugged terrain with dramatic elevation changes and exposed stone. Emerald ore generates here, and llamas can be found. Snow appears at high elevations. Waterfalls and floating terrain are common generation artifacts.

name: Windswept Forest
description: A forested variant of windswept hills with oak and spruce trees clinging to steep slopes. The combination of trees and extreme terrain makes navigation difficult. Emerald ore can be found underground.

name: Windswept Gravelly Hills
description: The surface layer is mostly gravel instead of grass, making it unstable and prone to collapse. Mining on steep slopes is risky due to gravel avalanches. Shares the emerald ore generation of other windswept biomes.

name: Windswept Savanna
description: Extremely chaotic terrain with massive overhangs, steep cliffs, and deep ravines. One of the most dramatic landscapes in the game. Coarse dirt and stone generate at the surface. No rain falls here despite the wild terrain.

name: Desert
description: Vast sandy terrain with no vegetation except cactus and dead bushes. Desert pyramids contain TNT traps and loot, husks replace zombies and inflict hunger, and camels spawn in desert villages.

name: Badlands
description: Striped terracotta hills with red sand valleys. Gold ore generates much more frequently here and at higher elevations than normal. Mineshafts generate at surface level. No rain falls, and the terrain is mostly barren.

name: Wooded Badlands
description: Badlands plateaus topped with coarse dirt and scattered oak trees. One of the rarest biomes in the game. Combines the enhanced gold ore of badlands with a wood source. Wolves spawn here unlike other badlands variants.

name: Eroded Badlands
description: Features dramatic terracotta spire formations that tower above narrow valleys, resembling real-world hoodoos. Very rare biome. Shares the enhanced gold ore generation with other badlands types. The terrain is extremely uneven.

name: Swamp
description: Shallow murky water with oak trees draped in vines and lily pads on the surface. Slimes spawn at night on the surface, and witches inhabit swamp huts. Blue orchids grow exclusively here. Frogs and bogged also spawn in this biome.

name: Mangrove Swamp
description: Dense mangrove trees with tangled root systems grow out of warm, shallow water and mud blocks. The only source of mangrove wood and mud. Frogs and tropical fish spawn here. Terrain is almost entirely waterlogged.

name: Taiga
description: Spruce forest with ferns and sweet berry bushes on the ground. Villages here use spruce wood architecture. Wolves and foxes spawn naturally. Sweet berries provide an easy early food source but damage players who walk through them.

name: Snowy Taiga
description: Snow-covered spruce forest with a frozen ground layer. Igloos can generate here, some hiding secret basement rooms with brewing stands. White foxes spawn instead of the red variant. Sweet berry bushes still grow under the snow.

name: Old Growth Pine Taiga
description: Towering pine trees with mossy boulders scattered across the forest floor. Podzol covers the ground instead of grass. Trail ruins generate underground. The large trees provide excellent wood yields per tree.

name: Old Growth Spruce Taiga
description: Features massive 2x2 spruce trees with thick trunks, the largest spruce trees in the game. Podzol and coarse dirt replace grass. Mossy boulders dot the landscape. Trail ruins can be found underground.

name: Snowy Plains
description: Flat, snow-covered terrain with few trees where villages and igloos generate. Strays replace some skeletons and drop slowness arrows, and polar bears roam the landscape. Water exposed to the sky freezes into ice.

name: Ice Spikes
description: Packed ice spikes tower up to 50 blocks high across a frozen landscape of packed ice and snow. The only biome where packed ice generates naturally in large quantities. Very rare and distinctive. No structures generate here.

name: Snowy Slopes
description: Snow-covered mountainsides found between mountain meadows and peaks. Goats and rabbits are the only passive mobs. Powder snow generates naturally here and can trap players who walk on it. Igloos can spawn on the slopes.

name: Frozen Peaks
description: Smooth, rounded mountain peaks covered in ice and packed ice with stone underneath. These form in areas where peaks border cold biomes. Only goats spawn here. The terrain is less jagged than jagged peaks but equally high.

name: Jagged Peaks
description: Sharp, steep mountain peaks with dramatic pointed summits reaching the highest elevations in the game. Stone and snow dominate the surface. Only goats can navigate the extreme terrain. Dangerous due to fall damage risk.

name: Stony Peaks
description: Warm-climate mountain peaks made of exposed stone, andesite, and calcite strips instead of snow. These generate when peaks border warm biomes. No passive mobs spawn here. Calcite is a unique decorative material found in these peaks.

name: Meadow
description: Elevated grassy plateau covered in flowers, generating on mountain slopes. Villages can spawn here at higher elevations than usual. A single tall flower always generates: either an allium or azure bluet. Good for dye farming.

name: Cherry Grove
description: Pink cherry blossom trees with falling petal particles cover gentle hillsides. The only source of cherry wood, which has a distinctive pink color. Generates on mountain slopes similar to meadows. The pink petals carpet the ground.

name: Grove
description: Snow-covered spruce forest found on mountain slopes between meadows and snowy slopes. Powder snow generates naturally and can trap players. Foxes and wolves inhabit these slopes. Functions as a transitional mountain biome.

name: Ocean
description: Standard ocean biome with a gravel and sand floor at moderate depth. Shipwrecks and ocean ruins can generate on the sea floor. Dolphins, cod, and squid swim in the water. Seagrass and kelp grow on the bottom.

name: Deep Ocean
description: Twice the depth of normal oceans with a floor around Y=30. Ocean monuments generate here, guarded by guardians and elder guardians. The depth makes underwater exploration more dangerous without water breathing.

name: Lukewarm Ocean
description: Warmer ocean with a sandy floor and sparse kelp. Tropical fish and pufferfish spawn alongside cod. Acts as a transition between normal and warm oceans. Coral does not generate here, but the aquatic life is more diverse.

name: Warm Ocean
description: Shallow tropical ocean with coral reefs and sea pickles on a sandy floor. The only biome where coral naturally generates. Tropical fish and pufferfish are abundant. No kelp or seagrass grows here.

name: Cold Ocean
description: Cold water ocean with a gravel floor. Salmon spawn here alongside cod. No tropical fish or coral reefs generate. The water appears darker and bluer than normal oceans.

name: Frozen Ocean
description: Ocean surface is covered with patches of ice and occasional large icebergs made of packed ice and blue ice. Polar bears spawn on the ice. Salmon replace cod in the cold water. Blue ice can be harvested from icebergs.

name: Deep Lukewarm Ocean
description: Deep variant of lukewarm ocean with ocean monuments. Combines the tropical fish diversity of lukewarm oceans with the depth and monument generation of deep oceans. Guardians patrol near monuments.

name: Deep Cold Ocean
description: Deep variant of cold ocean with ocean monuments and a gravel floor. Both cod and salmon spawn here. The combination of depth and cold makes it challenging to explore without proper enchantments.

name: Deep Frozen Ocean
description: Deep ocean with surface ice and icebergs, plus ocean monuments below. The surface ice makes it harder to access the depths. Icebergs extend from above the surface deep underwater. One of the more dangerous ocean variants.

name: River
description: Narrow waterway that cuts through terrain, connecting biomes. Salmon and squid spawn in the water. Useful for boat travel across the map. Sugar cane commonly grows along riverbanks, and clay generates on the riverbed.

name: Frozen River
description: River biome with a frozen surface that generates when rivers pass through snowy biomes. Players can walk across the ice, while salmon and drowned lurk beneath the frozen surface.

name: Beach
description: Sandy shoreline between land and ocean biomes. Turtles lay eggs on beaches, which are their home beach for life. Buried treasure chests contain hearts of the sea. Sugar cane frequently generates along the water edge.

name: Snowy Beach
description: Snow-covered sandy shoreline adjacent to cold biomes. Buried treasure can still generate here. No passive mobs spawn on the frozen sand. Water at the edge does not freeze due to the beach temperature being just above freezing.

name: Stony Shore
description: Rocky cliff face where mountains meet the ocean, made of stone and gravel instead of sand. No structures or passive mobs generate here. The steep terrain can create dramatic coastal cliffs. Useful source of stone near water.

name: Mushroom Fields
description: Rare island biome covered in mycelium where huge mushrooms grow like trees. No hostile mobs spawn on the surface, making it one of the safest biomes. Mooshrooms are exclusive to this biome and can be milked for mushroom stew.

name: Nether Wastes
description: The most common Nether biome, a vast expanse of netherrack with lava oceans. Nether quartz and nether gold ore generate throughout. Glowstone clusters hang from the ceiling. Both nether fortresses and bastion remnants can generate here.

name: Soul Sand Valley
description: Massive cavern biome floored with soul sand and soul soil, with blue soul fire burning throughout. Ghasts spawn more frequently here than other Nether biomes. Nether fossils made of bone blocks generate in the terrain. Skeletons replace piglins.

name: Crimson Forest
description: Red fungal forest with crimson nylium floor, huge crimson fungi trees, and weeping vine tendrils hanging from the ceiling. Hoglins and piglins spawn here, making it dangerous but a source of food via hoglin drops. Crimson wood is fireproof.

name: Warped Forest
description: Blue-green fungal forest with warped nylium, huge warped fungi, and twisting vines growing upward. The safest Nether biome since only endermen spawn here naturally. Warped wood is fireproof. Shroomlights provide natural light.

name: Basalt Deltas
description: Volcanic landscape of basalt columns and blackstone with magma blocks and lava pools. The uneven terrain with many gaps makes traversal dangerous. Magma cubes spawn very frequently here. One of the best sources of blackstone.

name: The End
description: The central End island where the ender dragon fight takes place. Made entirely of end stone with obsidian pillars topped with end crystals. After defeating the dragon, end gateways appear leading to the outer End islands.

name: End Highlands
description: Elevated outer End terrain where end cities and end ships generate. The only place to find elytra, shulker shells, and dragon heads. Chorus trees grow naturally here. Considered the most valuable End biome for loot.

name: End Midlands
description: Transitional End biome between the outer islands and highlands. End cities can generate here but are less common than in highlands. The terrain is flatter than highlands. Chorus trees still grow in this zone.

name: End Barrens
description: Barren end stone at the edges of outer End islands, typically bordering the void. No structures generate here. The terrain drops off sharply into the void, making fall deaths a constant risk. Only endermen spawn.

name: Small End Islands
description: Tiny floating end stone islands in the void between larger End landmasses. These small platforms offer little resources and high fall risk. No structures generate here. Bridging between islands requires caution.

name: The Void
description: An empty biome used by the Void superflat preset. Contains only a single stone platform at spawn. No natural generation occurs. Primarily used for creative mode building in superflat worlds.

name: Lush Caves
features: Azalea trees, moss, glow berries, dripleaf, spore blossoms, clay pools
description: Underground biome lush with moss, glow berries on cave vines, and dripleaf plants over clay pools. Azalea trees on the surface indicate lush caves below. Axolotls are exclusive to this biome and are useful combat allies underwater.

name: Deep Dark
features: Sculk blocks, sculk shriekers, sculk sensors, ancient cities
description: Generates deep underground (typically below Y=0) and is covered in sculk blocks. Sculk shriekers summon the warden, the most dangerous mob in the game. Ancient cities contain unique loot including echo shards and disc fragments. Avoid triggering sensors.

name: Dripstone Caves
features: Pointed dripstone, dripstone blocks, small water pools
description: Underground biome filled with stalactites and stalagmites made of pointed dripstone. Dripstone can be used to farm lava renewably with cauldrons. Small water pools with drowned can generate. The pointed dripstone deals extra fall damage.

name: Pale Garden
features: Pale oak trees, pale moss, hanging moss, eyeblossoms, creaking hearts
description: Eerie forest of pale oak trees draped in hanging pale moss with eyeblossoms that open at night. Creaking hearts hidden in trees spawn the creaking mob, which can only move when unobserved. The biome has a desaturated, unsettling atmosphere.
